name: Jimi Hendrix
abstract: James Marshall "Jimi" Hendrix (born Johnny Allen Hendrix; November 27, 1942 – September 18, 1970) was an American guitarist, singer, and songwriter. He is widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential guitarists of all time.

works_created.t: 1983… (A Merman I Should Turn to Be)

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Kininger
about_quotes.evidence: varying from Jimi Hendrix to Michael Jackson to Cannonball to Mozart... I would have loved to have seen Cannonball... And Jimi Hendrix. Yeah, you’d like to have been a part of that crowd.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Kininger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: We heard Jimi Hendrix. I always say – I tell you, if it had been a couple of years later, it would have been Jimi Hendrix, not Coltrane. That would have been, “This is my idol.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: I just remember him wearing hi-tops, having an electric guitar, and when it was time for him to solo it was like he gave Jimi Hendrix a run for the money.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe and Christian Goodwillie, via Zoom, June 30, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: You know in the car we’re more likely to be listening to like Zeppelin, or Jimi Hendrix, or The Beatles or Stevie Wonder or something like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Levin
about_quotes.evidence: Gil I think heard a lot of Jimi Hendrix in Hiram’s playing. Not the exact notes or the riffs just the spirit that Hiram plays with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Levin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph LaLama
about_quotes.evidence: I love Jimi Hendrix, you know, I loved a lot of people when I was growing up especially, that had something to say.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph LaLama interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 23, 2010
